blocks.text: through the middle of the street of the city. On either side of the river is the tree of life with its twelve kinds of fruit, producing its fruit each month; and the leaves of the tree are for the healing of the nations.
2 : , a collective reference to many trees (Ezek 47.12; see 2.7n.).
Nothing accursed will be found there any more. But the throne of God and of the Lamb will be in it, and his servants will worship him;
3 : is one throne, 3.21.
they will see his face, and his name will be on their foreheads.
4 : To God's , full awareness of God's immediate presence (Job 33.26; Ps 10.11; 42.2), an eschatological blessing (Ps 84.7; Mt 5.8; 1 Jn 3.2). , see 7.3n.; 14.1; contrast 13.16; 17.5.
And there will be no more night; they need no light of lamp or sun, for the Lord God will be their light, and they will reign forever and ever.
21.1–22.5: Vision of the new Jerusalem.
22.1–5: The river and the tree of life.
5 : Those who God (v. 3) with God in eternal triumph (Dan 7.18,27).
And he said to me, “These words are trustworthy and true, for the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, has sent his angel to show his servantsc what must soon take place.”
6 : , an angel (v. 8), perhaps the one referred to in 1.1. , the contents of this book. , the one who inspired the spirits of the prophets (1QH 20.11–13).
“See, I am coming soon! Blessed is the one who keeps the words of the prophecy of this book.”
7 : A parenthesis, reporting the words of Jesus (cf. 16.15). , see 1.3n.
I, John, am the one who heard and saw these things. And when I heard and saw them, I fell down to worship at the feet of the angel who showed them to me;
but he said to me, “You must not do that! I am a fellow servant with you and your comrades the prophets, and with those who keep the words of this book. Worship God!”
And he said to me, “Do not seal up the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is near.
10 : , cf. Isa 8.16; Dan 12.4. This book is to remain unsealed because the end is imminent.
Let the evildoer still do evil, and the filthy still be filthy, and the righteous still do right, and the holy still be holy.”
“See, I am coming soon; my reward is with me, to repay according to everyone's work.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.”
13 : Jesus applies God's title to himself (see 1.8n.).
Blessed are those who wash their robes, so that they will have the right to the tree of life and may enter the city by the gates.
14 : , the seventh beatitude of the book; see 1.3n. , 7.14. , the heavenly Jerusalem.
Outside are the dogs and sorcerers and fornicators and murderers and idolaters, and everyone who loves and practices falsehood.
15 : A list of those who are not permitted to enter the city, characterized generally as
“It is I, Jesus, who sent my angel to you with this testimony for the churches. I am the root and the descendant of David, the bright morning star.”
The Spirit and the bride say, “Come.” And let everyone who hears say, “Come.” And let everyone who is thirsty come. Let anyone who wishes take the water of life as a gift.
17 : , 21.2,9. , singular in Gk, is addressed to Jesus, as in v. 20.
I warn everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds to them, God will add to that person the plagues described in this book;
if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God will take away that person's share in the tree of life and in the holy city, which are described in this book.
18–19 : A stern warning against altering the teachings of (cf. Deut 4.2; 12.21).
The one who testifies to these things says, “Surely I am coming soon.” Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!
20 : , Jesus Christ; see 1.2. , a response, with liturgical echoes (1 Cor 16.22), to
The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all the saints. Amen.
22.6–21: Epilogue, consisting of warnings and exhortations.
21 : An epistolary postscript, corresponding to the epistolary salutation in 1.4–8.
